get.summary:
List files owned by the authenticated team, paginated. The response
always returns a `pagination_token`; pass it back as a query parameter
to fetch the next page. The end of the list is reached when the
returned `data` array is shorter than `limit`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of objects to be returned in a single response.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The ordering to sort the returned files. Use `asc` for ascending and `desc` for descending order.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: The field to sort by. Valid options: `created_at`, `filename`, `size`. Defaults to `created_at`.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token returned by the previous list files request.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Only included for compatibility. Use `pagination_token` instead.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
AIP-160 filter expression to narrow down results.

**Filterable fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` (or `file_name`) | string | Fuzzy match on filename |
| `file_id` | string | Exact match on file ID |
| `size_bytes` | integer | File size in bytes |
| `content_type` | string | Partial match on MIME type (e.g. `"pdf"` matches `"application/pdf"`) |
| `created_at` | timestamp | RFC 3339 timestamp (e.g. `"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"`) |
| `expires_at` | timestamp | RFC 3339 timestamp |
| `upload_status` | string | Upload status (`"Complete"`) |
| `user_defined_id` | string | Exact match on user-defined ID |

**Operators:** `=`, `!=`, `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`

**Logical:** `AND`, `OR`, `NOT`

**Examples:**
- `name:"quarterly report"` — fuzzy match on filename
- `content_type = "pdf"` — files with PDF content type
- `size_bytes > 1000000 AND created_at > "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"` — files larger than 1 MB created after Jan 1, 2024
- `file_id = "file_abc123"` — exact file ID match

post.summary:
Upload a file to xAI's storage. Returns the file's metadata. Files can
be referenced by ID anywhere a `file_id` is accepted (e.g. chat
attachments). Maximum file size: 50 MB. Files are kept until you
delete them, or until `expires_after` elapses if set at upload time.

400.description: Bad request. Common causes: `expires_after` outside [3600, 2592000], `expires_after` field appearing after `file` in the multipart body, missing or unnamed `file` part, invalid filename.

413.description: Payload too large -- file exceeds 50 MB.

post.summary: Compacts a full Responses API input window into a shorter canonical window.
post.description:
The client sends its current input (the same items that would be passed
to `POST /v1/responses`) and receives a compacted output window.
The output should be used **verbatim** as the `input` of the next
`/v1/responses` call (appending only the new user turn).

This generalizes the compaction approach used by the coding-agent
harness (`generate_session_compact` in xai-grok-shell):

1. Strip tool-result noise from the history.
2. Ask the model to produce a structured `<summary>` of the conversation.
3. Rebuild a compact window:  system message → summary → last user query.
4. Return that window to the client.

summary.text:
First, the user asked: "What is 101*3?"

This is a simple multiplication: 101 multiplied by 3.

Calculating: 100 * 3 = 300, and 1 * 3 = 3, so 300 + 3 = 303.

I should respond helpfully and directly, as per my system prompt: "You are a helpful assistant that can answer questions and help with tasks."

Keep the response concise and accurate. No need for extra fluff unless it adds value.

Final answer: 303.

get.summary: Get the result of a deferred video generation request.
get.description:
Returns the current status of a video generation job. When the job completes
successfully the response contains the generated video URL. When the job fails
the response contains a structured `error` object with a machine-readable
`code` and a human-readable `message`.

Both successful and failed completions return HTTP 200 — use the `status`
field (`"done"` or `"failed"`) to distinguish between the two.

parameters.name: request_id
parameters.description: The deferred request id returned by a previous video generation request.

200.description: Video generation completed or failed. Check the `status` field to determine the outcome.

400.description: Bad request. The request is invalid or an invalid API key is provided.

404.description: Not found. No deferred video could be found with the given request_id.

ChatRequest.description: The chat request body for `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint.

deferred.description: If set to `true`, the request returns a `request_id`. You can then get the deferred response by GET `/v1/chat/deferred-completion/{request_id}`.

frequency_penalty.description: (Not supported by reasoning models) Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

logit_bias.description: (Unsupported) A JSON object that maps tokens (specified by their token ID in the tokenizer) to an associated bias value from -100 to 100. Mathematically, the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling. The exact effect will vary per model, but values between -1 and 1 should decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100 should result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token.

logprobs.description: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the content of message. Not supported by models `grok-4.20` and newer; the field will be silently ignored if set.

max_completion_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, only applies to visible output tokens (i.e. does not apply to tokens used for reasoning or function calls). Defaults to None, meaning the model will generate as many tokens as needed up until the model's maximum context length.

max_tokens.description: \[DEPRECATED\] The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion. Deprecated in favor of `max_completion_tokens`.

messages.description: A list of messages that make up the chat conversation. Different models support different message types, such as image and text.

model.description: Model name for the model to use. Obtainable from <https://console.x.ai/team/default/models> or <https://docs.x.ai/docs/models>.

n.description: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message. Note that you will be charged based on the number of generated tokens across all of the choices. Keep n as 1 to minimize costs.

parallel_tool_calls.description: If set to false, the model can perform maximum one tool call.

presence_penalty.description: (Not supported by `grok-3` and reasoning models) Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

reasoning_effort.description: Constrains how hard a reasoning model thinks before responding. Only supported by `grok-4.3`. Possible values are `none` (disables reasoning completely), `low` (this is the default if not specified), `medium` and `high` (uses the most reasoning tokens).

oneOf.description: An object specifying the format that the model must output. Specify `{ "type": "json_object" }` for JSON output, or `{ "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} }` for structured outputs. If `{ \"type\": \"text\" }`, the model will return a text response.
Set the parameters to be used for searched data. If not set, no data will be acquired by the model.

seed.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same `seed` and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed, and you should refer to the `system_fingerprint` response parameter to monitor changes in the backend.

oneOf.description:
Specifies the processing tier for this request. Set to `"priority"` for higher scheduling
priority at a higher token price. Valid values: `"auto"` (default), `"priority"`.

stop.description: (Not supported by reasoning models) Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens.

stream.description: If set, partial message deltas will be sent. Tokens will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available, with the stream terminated by a `data: [DONE]` message.

oneOf.description: Options for streaming response. Only set this when you set `stream: true`.

temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.

oneOf.description: Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model. `none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message. auto means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools. required means the model must call one or more tools. Specifying a particular tool via `{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "my_function"}}` forces the model to call that tool. `none` is the default when no tools are present. `auto` is the default if tools are present.

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call in JSON-schema. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. Use this to provide a list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for. A max of 128 functions are supported.

top_logprobs.description: An integer between 0 and 8 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability. logprobs must be set to true if this parameter is used. Not supported by models `grok-4.20` and newer; the field will be silently ignored if set.

top_p.description: An alternative to sampling with `temperature`, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with `top_p` probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. It is generally recommended to alter this or `temperature` but not both.

user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help xAI to monitor and detect abuse.

oneOf.description:
Options to control the web search. This is only included for compatibility reason. Prefer
the usage of `realtime_data_parameters` instead.

CompactRequest.description:
The request body for `POST /v1/responses/compact`.

Clients send the full current input window (the same items they would
pass to `/v1/responses`) and receive back a compacted window suitable
for use as the `input` of the next `/v1/responses` call.

input.description: The full input window to compact.

model.description: Model to use for compaction summarization (required).

CompactResponse.description:
The response body for `POST /v1/responses/compact`.

The `output` list contains a single compaction item wrapping the entire
compacted conversation in an encrypted blob. Clients should spread
`output` into their next `/v1/responses` input:

```python
next_input = [*compacted.output, new_user_msg]
```

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the compacted conversation was created.

model.description: Model used for the compaction summary.

object.description: Always `"response.compaction"`.

output.description:
Compacted output containing a single compaction item.
Pass this verbatim as input to the next `/v1/responses` call.

oneOf.description: Token usage information for the compaction call.

CompactUsage.description:
Token usage details for a compaction call.

Mirrors OpenAI's `ResponseUsage` shape (`input_tokens`,
`input_tokens_details.cached_tokens`, `output_tokens`,
`output_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`, `total_tokens`) and adds
`dropped_message_count` as an xAI-specific extension.

dropped_message_count.description: Number of messages dropped/summarized during compaction.

input_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the original (pre-compaction) input.

output_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the compacted output.

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used (input + output, including reasoning).

CompactUsageInputTokensDetails.description: Breakdown of input tokens for a compaction call.

cached_tokens.description: Number of input tokens that were served from the prompt cache.

CompactUsageOutputTokensDetails.description: Breakdown of output tokens for a compaction call.

reasoning_tokens.description: Number of reasoning tokens generated during compaction.

CompactionOutputItem.description:
A compaction item from a previous `/v1/responses/compact` call.

Accepted as input in `/v1/responses` calls. Clients must treat
`encrypted_content` as opaque — do not parse or depend on its
internal structure.

encrypted_content.description: The encrypted content of the compacted conversation.

id.description: The unique ID of the compaction item (e.g. `cmp_<uuid>`).

type.description: The type of the item. Always `"compaction"`.

filter.description:
AIP-160 filter expression to narrow down results.

**Filterable fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` (or `file_name`) | string | Fuzzy match on filename |
| `file_id` | string | Exact match on file ID |
| `size_bytes` | integer | File size in bytes |
| `content_type` | string | Partial match on MIME type (e.g. `"pdf"` matches `"application/pdf"`) |
| `created_at` | timestamp | RFC 3339 timestamp (e.g. `"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"`) |
| `expires_at` | timestamp | RFC 3339 timestamp |
| `upload_status` | string | Upload status (`"Complete"`) |
| `user_defined_id` | string | Exact match on user-defined ID |

**Operators:** `=`, `!=`, `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`

**Logical:** `AND`, `OR`, `NOT`

**Examples:**
- `name:"quarterly report"` — fuzzy match on filename
- `content_type = "pdf"` — files with PDF content type
- `size_bytes > 1000000 AND created_at > "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"` — files larger than 1 MB created after Jan 1, 2024
- `file_id = "file_abc123"` — exact file ID match

function.description: Definition of tool call available to the model.

Tool.description: Definition of one tool that the model can call.

function.description: Function to call for the tool call.

id.description: A unique ID of the tool call generated by xAI. After performing tool call's function, user provides this ID with tool call's result in the subsequent request to xAI. xAI can then match the tool call result sent with tool call request.

type.description: Type of tool call, should be `"function"` or `"web_search_call"` or `"x_search_call"` or `"code_interpreter_call"` or `"mcp_call"`

oneOf.description: Controls tool access by the model. `"none"` makes model ignore tools, `"auto"` let the model automatically decide whether to call a tool, `"required"` forces model to pick a tool to call.
Name of the function to use.

type.description: Type is always `"function"`.

ToolChoice.description: Parameter to control how model chooses the tools.

oneOf.description: Plain text content.
An array of content blocks (text, image, etc.).

ToolResultContent.description:
Content of a tool_result block. The Anthropic SDK may send this as either a plain
string or an array of typed content blocks (e.g. `[{"type": "text", "text": "..."}]`).

oneOf.description: Text content block.
Image content block.

ToolResultContentBlock.description: A single content block within a tool_result's array content.

bytes.description: The ASCII encoding of the output character.

logprob.description: The log probability of returning this token.

UploadFileMultipartRequest.description:
Multipart form fields accepted by `POST /v1/files`. The `expires_after`
field MUST appear before `file` in the multipart body when both are
present (the upload handler streams `file` straight to gRPC and cannot
rewind to read metadata after).

expires_after.description:
Optional TTL in seconds (measured from upload time). Must be between
3600 (1 hour) and 2592000 (30 days). If unset the file does not expire.

Accepts either a plain integer or the OpenAI SDK deepObject form
(`expires_after[anchor]=created_at` + `expires_after[seconds]=N`)
as separate multipart fields. The anchor+seconds form must arrive
before the `file` part.

file.description:
The file to upload. The filename from the multipart
`Content-Disposition: filename=` header is recorded as the file's
`filename`.

purpose.description:
Optional purpose label, accepted for OpenAI SDK compatibility. xAI
does not enforce or interpret this field. Setting `"assistants"`
is the conventional choice.

files.description:
Skill zip file or one file from a directory upload. Clients may send
this field once with a zip file, or repeatedly with directory files.

completion_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of completion token usage of different types.

cost_in_usd_ticks.description:
Accurate cost of this request in USD ticks, where "tick" is defined as follows:
TICKS_IN_USD_CENT: i64 = 100_000_000
which means there is 10'000'000'000 ticks in one *dollar*.

num_sources_used.description: Number of individual live search source used.

prompt_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of prompt token usage of different types.

total_tokens.description: Total token used, the sum of prompt token and completion token amount.

VideoError.description: Structured error returned when video generation fails.

code.description:
Machine-readable error code indicating the type of failure.
See `VideoErrorCode` for all possible values.

message.description: Human-readable error message describing the failure.

VideoErrorCode.description:
Machine-readable error codes for video generation failures.

These are the codes that can appear in `VideoError.code` when polling a
deferred video generation result. Authentication, model-not-found, and
synchronous rate-limit errors are returned as HTTP errors and never
appear in `VideoError`. Engine overload encountered mid-generation
surfaces here as `service_unavailable` (HTTP 503).

Serializes to/from snake_case strings (e.g. `"invalid_argument"`,
`"internal_error"`) for JSON compatibility.

VideoGenerationModel.description: Details of a video generation model

aliases.description: Alias ID(s) of the model that user can use in a request's model field.

created.description: Model creation time in Unix timestamp.

fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the xAI system configuration hosting the model.

input_modalities.description: The input modalities supported by the model (e.g. "text", "image").

object.description: The object type, which is always `"model"`.

output_modalities.description: The output modalities supported by the model (e.g. "video").

upload_url.description: Signed URL to upload the generated video via HTTP PUT.

oneOf.description:
Structured error describing why video generation failed.
Only present when the generation encountered a failure (4xx or 5xx).

model.description: The model used to generate the video. Omitted when status is "failed".

progress.description:
Approximate completion percentage for the video generation task (0-100).
- When status is "pending": progress is between 0-99, indicating current completion.
- When status is "done": progress is 100.
- When status is "failed": progress is omitted.

status.description: Status of the video generation: "done" when the video is ready.

oneOf.description: Billing and cost information for this request.
The generated video data. Omitted when status is "failed".

VideoUrl.description:
Video input for editing and extension requests.
Accepts a public URL, a base64-encoded data URL, or a file_id from the xAI Files API.

file_id.description:
File ID from the xAI Files API. Mutually exclusive with `url`.
The file must be a video (e.g., MP4) and fully uploaded.

url.description:
URL of the video (public URL or base64-encoded data URL).
The video must have the `.mp4` file extension and be encoded with `.mp4` supported codecs such as H.265, H.264, AV1, etc.
Required when `file_id` is not set.

oneOf.description: Action type "search" - Performs a web search query.

sources.description: The sources used in the search.

oneOf.description: Action type "open_page" - Opens a specific URL from search results.

url.description: The URL of the page to open.

oneOf.description:
Action type "find_in_page": Searches for a pattern within a loaded
page. Wire shape mirrors OpenAI's `ActionFind` so the OpenAI Python
SDK's typed `web_search_call.action` parses correctly — note the
`find_in_page` tag (not `find`) and the flat `url` (not nested
under a `source` field).

pattern.description: The pattern or text to search for within the page.

url.description: The URL of the page being searched within.

WebSearchCall.description: The output of a web search tool call.

action.description:
An object describing the specific action taken in this web search call.
Includes details on how the model used the web (search, open_page, find).

id.description: The unique ID of the web search tool call.

status.description: The status of the web search tool call.

type.description: The type of the web search tool call. Always `web_search_call`.

allowed_domains.description:
List of website domains (without protocol specification or subdomains)
to restrict search results to (e.g., ["example.com"]). A maximum of 5 websites can be allowed.
Use this as a whitelist to limit results to only these specific sites; no other websites will
be considered. If no relevant information is found on these websites, the number of results
returned might be smaller than `max_search_results` set in `SearchParameters`. Note: This
parameter cannot be set together with `excluded_domains`.

excluded_domains.description:
List of website domains (without protocol specification or subdomains) to exclude from search results (e.g., ["example.com"]).
Use this to prevent results from unwanted sites. A maximum of 5 websites can be excluded.
This parameter cannot be set together with `allowed_domains`.

search_context_size.description: This field included for compatibility reason with OpenAI's API. It is mapped to `max_search`.

type.description: The type of source.

country.description: Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, like US, GB, etc.

timezone.description: Timezone of the user's location, IANA timezone like America/Chicago, Europe/London, etc.

type.description: Type is always `"approximate"`.

ZdrStatus.description: Zero Data Retention status for a team.

PublicUrlOptions.description: Permissive placeholder added because the official xAI OpenAPI spec references PublicUrlOptions but does not define it.

servers.description: xAI API base URL from official REST API reference
